regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces unprecedented simultaneous crises: external military pressure (US blockade with 31+ interceptions, Israeli strikes on Hezbollah, IRGC attacks on shipping) combined with the most serious domestic unrest in regime history, security force defections, and a hardening US stance rejecting Iran's ceasefire framework. Internal regime cohesion shows contradictory signals—high-level officials participating in diplomacy while the IRGC acknowledges defections, security forces are refusing orders, and the regime deploys foreign militias to conduct brutal crackdowns on protesters.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Between April 27-28, 2026, Trump canceled US-Iran peace talks in Pakistan while simultaneously issuing a 3-day ultimatum and convening security meetings on military options; Iran's military declared the war with the US and Israel has not ended despite ceasefire, continued target database updates, and escalated attacks on commercial shipping; and the regime deployed nearly 5,000 foreign militia fighters to suppress the most serious protests in its history while arresting security forces who refused to fire on civilians.
